body.title: Installer — owner of GLOBAL / cross-instance init + provisioning
body.summary: The installer device owns GLOBAL instance-init and provisioning: standing up a fresh UU instance, seeding cross-instance shared state, and deploying managed skills. It instantiates factory specs (factory.py: load_spec -> instantiate -> health_rollup/report_eval/escalate/halt, wiring member manifests and announcing to the factory owner), and deploys managed skills as symlinks from the master repo skills/<name> into the target ~/.claude/skills/<name> (backends.py — never a copy, which drifts; manifest.py is the source of truth for which skills are managed). Per D-installer-absorbs-init-2026-06-28 the installer ALSO absorbs global init that was previously ownerless and scattered (global_kb seed/loader, shared-table DDL). THE BOUNDARY (load-bearing): installer owns GLOBAL init only — seeding global_kb, creating shared Postgres tables, provisioning a fresh instance. DEVICE-LOCAL bootstrap (a device standing up its OWN state, e.g. igor/acquisition/bootstrap.py) STAYS with that device — pulling it into installer would break blast-radius containment (a broken device bootstrap could then crash global provisioning). Global-vs-device-local is the discriminator: does this code seed/provision CROSS-instance shared state (-> installer) or initialize ONE device's own state (-> stays device-local)?

key_concepts.name: global init vs device-local init
key_concepts.what: installer owns cross-instance/shared init (global_kb seed, shared-table DDL, fresh-instance provisioning); each device keeps its own bootstrap for blast-radius containment. The boundary is the discriminator for what may move under installer.

key_concepts.name: factory instantiation
key_concepts.what: factory.py loads a factory YAML spec, wires member MemberManifests with the factory channel, and posts FACTORY_ANNOUNCE/HEALTH/EVAL/ESCALATE/HALT to the owner comms:// address (humans and agents are both just comms:// endpoints)

key_concepts.name: skill deploy = symlink, never copy
key_concepts.what: backends.py links skills/<name> -> ~/.claude/skills/<name>; manifest.py lists which skills are managed (unlisted local skills survive deploys). Copies drift; a link always resolves to the one canonical source.

key_concepts.name: global_kb (pending relocation)
key_concepts.what: global instance-init: clones/pulls a KB repo, creates global_kb.patterns (Postgres), upserts patterns/*.jsonl seed. Currently at unseen_university/global_kb.py + repo-root global_kb/ seed; moves under installer in T-home-global-kb-under-installer.

implementing_files: unseen_university/devices/installer/global_kb.py (global init loader; baseline seed at devices/installer/global_kb_seed/) — unwired, see T-global-kb-wire-and-legacy-env
